LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEMS (CALPERS), KENNETH MARZION, INDIVIDUALLY AND IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY, LORI MCGARTLAND, INDIVIDUALLY, SHAREN B. SCOTT, INDIVIDUALLY, MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES. HEARING HELD. AFTER NOTING PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS ON THE RECORD, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES GRANTED. THE CLAIMS AGAINST THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS WERE DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE IN FEDERAL COURT TWO MONTHS BEFORE THIS STATE ACTION WAS FILED. DEFENDANTS PREVAILED IN THIS COURT ON THE 42 U.S.C. SECTION 1983 CLAIMS WHEN THEY WERE DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE AS RES JUDICATA. THE CLAIMS BROUGHT IN STATE COURT WERE MERITLESS AND WITHOUT FOUNDATION. THE COURT USES THE LODESTAR ANALYSIS IN AWARDING DEFENDANTS REASONABLE AND NECESSARY FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $36,051. THE COURT DISALLOWS FEES FOR THE ANTI-SLAPP AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES ANALYSIS, AND FOR A PORTION OF THE FEES ATTRIBUTED TO THIS FEE MOTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM (CALPERS), KENNETH MARZION, LORI MCGARTLAND, AND, SHAREN SCOTT DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF DUE PROCESS AND 42 U.S.C. 1983. THESE CLAIMS WERE BROUGHT IN THE FEDERAL COURT ACTION AGAINST THE SAME INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS AND DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE IN MARCH 2009. THE PRIMARY RIGHTS DOCTRINE BARS PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS IN THE CURRENT SUIT. NO SHOWING THAT PUBLIC POLICY AND INJUSTICE EXCEPTIONS TO RES JUDICATA WOULD APPLY. THE COURT ADDS: JUDGMENT MAY BE ENTERED FOR INDIVIDUALS IN SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEMS (CALPERS), KENNETH MARZION, INDIVIDUALLY AND IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY, LORI MCGARTLAND, INDIVIDUALLY, SHAREN B. SCOTT, INDIVIDUALLY, (AMENDED) MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED. THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO STATE A CLAIM THAT THE REGULATIONS AND ADMINISTRATIVE FRAMEWORK ARE UNCONSTITUTIONAL AS APPLIED. WRIT REVIEW IS AVAILABLE POST-HEARING. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO SHOW HOW THE TOTALITY OF THE ADMINISTRATIVE RIGHTS AVAILABLE TO PLAINTIFF DOES NOT COMPORT WITH DUE PROCESS. THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION ALSO FAILS TO STATE A CLAIM THAT THE REGULATIONS ARE FACIALLY UNCONSTITUTIONAL. A HEARING MUST BE HELD WITHIN A "REASONABLE" TIME WHEN NOT FIXED BY STATUTE, AND PLAINTIFFS HAVE A RIGHT TO IMMEDIATE WRIT REVIEW WHERE A HEARING IS NOT HELD WITHIN A REASONABLE TIME. NO EXHAUSTION OF ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES DEMONSTRATED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

(Amended)Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Notice
Off Calendar Jan-12-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEMS (CalPERS), KENNETH MARZION, individually and in his official capacity, LORI MCGARTLAND, individually, SHAREN B. SCOTT, individually, (Amended) Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Appearances: Scott N. Kivel, Esq. Attorney at Law, (707) 762-5526 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Jeffrey Ryan Rieger, Esq. of ReedSmith LLP (510) 466-6870 appearing for CalPers. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted. The first cause of action fails to state a claim that the regulations and administrative framework are unconstitutional as applied. Writ review is available post-hearing. Plaintiffs fail to show how the totality of the administrative rights available to plaintiff does not comport with due process. The first cause of action also fails to state a claim that the regulations are facially unconstitutional. A hearing must be held within a "reasonable" time when not fixed by statute, and plaintiffs have a right to immediate writ review where a hearing is not held within a reasonable time. No exhaustion of administrative remedies demonstrated. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attorney Fees; Req. For Judicial Ntc And Index Of Non Calif. Authorities
Off Calendar Jan-12-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEMS (CalPERS), KENNETH MARZION, individually and in his official capacity, LORI MCGARTLAND, individually, SHAREN B. SCOTT, individually, Motion For Attorney Fees. Appearances: Scott N. Kivel, Esq. Attorney at Law, (707) 762-5526 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Jeffrey Ryan Rieger, Esq. of ReedSmith LLP (510) 466-6870 appearing for CalPERS. Hearing held. After noting plaintiff's objections on the record, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for attorney fees granted. The claims against the individual defendants were dismissed with prejudice in federal court two months before this state action was filed. Defendants prevailed in this Court on the 42 U.S.C. section 1983 claims when they were dismissed with prejudice as res judicata. The claims brought in state Court were meritless and without foundation. The court uses the lodestar analysis in awarding defendants reasonable and necessary fees in the amount of $36,051. The court disallows fees for the anti-Slapp and punitive damages analysis, and for a portion of the fees attributed to this fee motion. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-28-2009 Continued To Nov-25-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Sep-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM (CALPERS), KENNETH MARZION, LORI MCGARTLAND, and, SHAREN SCOTT DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT. Appearances: Scott N. Kivel, Esq. Law Office of Scott N. Kivel (707) 762-5526, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Harvey L. Leiderman, Esq. and Jeffery Rieger, Esq. of ReedSmith LLP (415) 659-5914, appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to the first cause of action for declaratory relief. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action for violation of due process and 42 U.S.C. 1983. These claims were brought in the federal court action against the same individual defendants and dismissed with prejudice in March 2009. The primary rights doctrine bars plaintiffs' claims in the current suit. No showing that public policy and injustice exceptions to res judicata would apply. The Court adds: Judgment may be entered for individuals in second and third causes of action. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
